name: PLS Check Cashers
description_short: PLS Check Cashers is a 24/7 financial services chain offering check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, vehicle registration, and prepaid debit cards across 12 states.
description_long:
PLS Check Cashers, operating under the brand PLS 247 and owned by PLS Financial Services, Inc., is a multi-state check cashing and financial services chain with locations across Arizona, California, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Massachusetts, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Texas, and Wisconsin. The company operates as a Community Currency Exchange in Illinois, a state-licensed designation that authorizes a broader range of financial transactions than typical check cashers.

PLS offers an unusually wide service mix for a check cashing operator. Core financial services include check cashing, money orders, bill payment, government benefits processing, and foreign currency exchange. International remittances are handled through Ria Money Transfer, one of the largest global money transfer networks. PLS also issues its own prepaid debit product — the Xpectations! PLUS Debit Mastercard — along with a companion mobile app, giving unbanked customers a reloadable card option. A Toys for Tots co-branded version of the card is also available.

What sets PLS apart from most check cashers is its Illinois vehicle services counter: customers can handle car registration renewals, title transfers, license plate issuance, and city stickers at the same location as their financial transactions. This DMV-adjacent capability is specific to Illinois Community Currency Exchanges and is uncommon nationally. All services at the Chicago location are listed as open 24/7, making PLS one of the few financial service providers accessible at any hour. Ancillary services include notary services, fax and copy, ATMs, phone top-ups, and coin/cash exchange.

PLS fills a genuine need for consumers without bank accounts or those who need immediate cash from a check outside banking hours. However, check cashing fees — which PLS does not disclose publicly on its website — are typically 1–5% of the check amount, making repeated use significantly more expensive than a bank account over time. The 24/7 model and one-stop convenience are real advantages, but PLS is not a path to building credit or long-term financial health.
pros: Open 24/7 — all services available around the clock, including weekends and holidays
Vehicle registration, title transfers, and license plate services available on-site (Illinois locations)
Ria Money Transfer integration for international remittances to a wide global network
Xpectations! PLUS Debit Mastercard provides a prepaid banking alternative for unbanked customers
Foreign currency exchange available alongside standard money services
Notary services on-site for legal documents including wills, deeds, and power of attorney
Operates in 12 states with a consistent service menu across locations
cons: Check cashing fees are not disclosed on the website — customers cannot compare costs before visiting
No loan or credit products offered; does not address borrowing needs
Repeated use for paycheck cashing is materially more expensive than maintaining a bank account
No FDIC insurance or interest-bearing accounts — not a substitute for banking
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate cash from checks
Illinois residents who need vehicle registration or title transfer services outside DMV hours
Immigrants or workers sending money internationally via Ria Money Transfer
Anyone needing after-hours financial services, bill payment, or notarization
diagnosis: PLS is best suited for unbanked consumers who need immediate check-to-cash conversion, international money transfers, or Illinois vehicle services outside normal business hours — all available 24/7 at a single location. The main caveat is cost: check cashing fees are not published, and frequent use for payroll deposits will erode significantly more income than a basic bank account would.
